industry: Digital Infrastructure / Bitcoin Mining & AI Data Centers
thesis: Core Scientific is pivoting from low-margin bitcoin mining to higher-margin AI/HPC colocation. HDC revenue grew 168% YoY in FY2025 to $65.4M, while total revenue declined 38% to $319M as mining shrank. The company operates ~745 MW of power capacity and ~19 EH/s of hash rate. The failed CoreWeave merger leaves it independent but with a credible growth narrative. Balance sheet is fragile (negative book value, ~$1.06B debt, ~$1.32B warrant liabilities), and operating profitability remains elusive. The stock trades at ~28x sales, pricing in a successful HDC pivot. High risk/reward.

drivers: HDC revenue scales to $300M+ by 2027 with high margins
New AI hyperscaler customer wins beyond CoreWeave
Bitcoin price rally improves mining cash flow and reduces pivot urgency
Block 3nm ASIC deployment adds 15 EH/s of efficient mining capacity

risks: HDC growth stalls; mining revenue collapses faster than expected
Cash burn forces dilutive equity raise at depressed prices
CoreWeave reduces HDC commitments or delays expansion
Material weakness in internal controls leads to further restatements

base_case.assumptions: HDC grows to $150–200M by 2027; mining revenue flat at ~$250M; operating losses narrow but persist until 2028; company avoids dilutive raise by using CoreWeave-funded capex and existing liquidity.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — HDC revenue growth and guidance update

key_risks: Customer concentration: CoreWeave dominates HDC revenue
Balance sheet fragility: negative book value, heavy debt, warrant overhang
Execution risk on HDC pivot: capex-intensive, competitive, unproven at scale
Bitcoin price and network difficulty volatility affecting mining economics
Material weakness in internal controls over financial reporting
